tipRules.tip: Yamamoto: 'Use a 3/0 Gamakatsu EWG hook — bigger hooks lose tail action on the fall and tear the bait off more often'
Yamamoto: 'Use smaller light-wire hooks to increase tail action and make the Senko last longer since bait slides up the hook onto the line while fighting fish'
Yamamoto: 'Tie with a Snell knot — when you tug on it, it pulls the hook barb straight toward the line for better hooksets'
Yamamoto: 'Use a simple lift-and-drop retrieve, like working a plastic worm but slower because it takes longer to fall'
Yamamoto: 'Scale down to 4-inch Senko in gin-clear water'
Yamamoto: 'Use 7-foot light-action spinning rod with 2500 series reel, 10-15lb braid mainline with fluorocarbon leader'

knowledge.insight: Yamamoto: 'I designed the Senko after trying the Slug-Go which I thought was too hard and bulky. The softer plastic and the way water flows around the lure as it falls gives it a continual vibration on a weightless Texas rig'
Yamamoto: Use 10-14lb Sugoi Fluorocarbon leader for stained water, 6-10lb for clear water
Yamamoto: 'Salt-and-pepper is probably a sleeper color — a forgotten color, but I can take it any place in the world and fish will bite it'
